mood: Editorial magazine, The Hoxton meets Vivi Bistrot, organic, chic, premium, watercolor-focused

scales.quote: text-3xl sm:text-4xl italic font-heading text-primary leading-snug

rules: Headings must use tight tracking and line-height to feel like a printed magazine.
Use bold italics (e.g., Cormorant Garamond Italic) for emphasis within headings to create typographic tension.
Overlines must be used for section numbering (e.g., 'N° 01 — IL DEHORS').

grids.asymmetrical_feature: grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-12 gap-12 lg:gap-24 items-start

layout_and_spacing.spacing_scale: Use generous padding. Sections should have py-24 or py-32. Elements should breathe.
rules: Avoid perfect symmetry. If left has text, right should have a larger image or negative space.
Use thin 1px borders (border-light) to delineate sections and columns, mimicking printed magazine layouts.
Do NOT use standard Shadcn card containers with heavy shadows. Use flat, borderless or thin-bordered bento boxes.

MagazineHeader.structure: Fixed top, backdrop-blur-xl bg-[#F7F4EB]/80, 1px bottom border.
MagazineHeader.layout: Flex container. Logo left, Nav links center, CTA (Reserve) right.
MagazineHeader.typography: Nav links should be sans-serif text-sm tracking-wide. Hover state: italic serif.

EditorialHero.layout: Left 7 cols: Massive H1, small practical info (colophon) at bottom left. Right 5 cols: Large immersive image bleeding off the right edge.
EditorialHero.features: No centered logo circle. Use a stark, bold typographic approach.

NumberedSection.structure: Grid layout. A massive serif number ('01') on the left or top, accompanied by a thin dividing line, followed by the content.

ChefDiary.structure: 2-column layout. Left: high-contrast image or watercolor. Right: Large italic pull quote, body text, and a hand-drawn stylistic signature.

MenuGallery.structure: Magazine-style list with dotted leaders for prices. Floating watercolor illustrations positioned absolutely or in a masonry layout alongside the text.

CustomCursor.appearance: A small terracotta dot (bg-primary) that expands on interactive elements.
CustomCursor.behavior: Hidden on mobile. Follows mouse on desktop.

motion_and_interactions.page_transitions: Use framer-motion for subtle fade and slide-up on page load.
motion_and_interactions.text_reveal: Staggered line-by-line reveal for massive headings (H1/H2).
motion_and_interactions.hover_effects: Text links should transition to italic serif on hover (e.g., hover:italic hover:font-heading). Images should have a very slow, subtle scale effect (duration-700) on hover.
motion_and_interactions.illustrations: Watercolor illustrations should have a subtle floating animation (up and down 10px over 6 seconds) to feel alive.

rules: Highlight the existing watercolor illustrations. They are the signature element.
Do NOT use heavy, dark photography. Keep images light, cinematic, and organic.

hero_dehors.description: Lush green outdoor seating, chic umbrella, perfect for the Dehors section or Hero.

interior_bistro.description: Whimsical green cafe interior, matches the organic earthy theme.

terrace_seating.description: Cafe with outdoor seating and ornate lanterns, editorial style.

testing.data_testid_rules: All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a data-testid attribute (e.g., data-testid='hero-reservation-button', data-testid='menu-category-link').

instructions_to_main_agent: Prioritize the Landing Page and Menu Page.
Ensure typography matches the Hoxton-inspired editorial feel: massive headings, small caps colophons, thin dividers.
Do NOT center everything. Push for asymmetrical 7-col/5-col splits.
Incorporate the client's watercolor illustrations natively, making them float alongside or overlap typography.
Implement the hover:italic micro-interaction on navigational links and buttons.
Setup Tailwind config with the custom colors, fonts (Cormorant Garamond, DM Sans), and extra large text sizes.
